title: erica tenuipilosa subsp. spicata
intro: Erica tenuipilosa subsp. spicata, also known as the Spiked Heath, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family Ericaceae. The Spiked Heath is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that may help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as support respiratory health. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin irritations, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties, which can help to clear congestion and promote wound healing. Erica tenuipilosa subsp. spicata can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural defenses.
